meta_title: For Freelancers: API integration guide to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: Master API Integration to Ship Faster
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on stalled projects. Learn to master API integration using Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver client work at record speed.
content.answer_block: To overcome project paralysis, shift from manual coding to AI-assisted API orchestration. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you automate the integration lifecycle, reducing boilerplate by 80%. This streamlined workflow ensures you ship robust, client-ready features without getting bogged down in implementation details.
content.intro:
The blinking cursor is the graveyard of ambition. You have the vision, the client's deposit, and the deadline looming, yet you're stuck in the mire of endless documentation and brittle API connections. It’s not a lack of skill; it’s a lack of leverage. When you manually wire every endpoint, you aren't building—you're just plumbing, and plumbing is where projects go to die.

Today, we stop the cycle of unfinished work. We’re moving to an AI-augmented development stack that treats integration as a configuration task rather than a coding chore. By combining the structural power of Antigravity with the autonomous execution of Claude Code, you will transform from a coder struggling with endpoints into an architect orchestrating complex systems in minutes.

steps.title: Define the Integration Schema
steps.description: Before writing code, formalize your API data requirements to prevent scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, type-safe schema definition for a RESTful API integration focusing on error handling and data transformation layers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Create a Zod schema based on the provided API documentation for the project endpoints.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Service Layer
steps.description: Create a decoupled service layer to ensure your business logic doesn't leak into your API calls.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular service class that handles authentication headers and base URL configuration for the target API.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Implement a service layer that uses Axios with interceptors for automatic token refresh.'

steps.title: Implement Resilient Retries
steps.description: API failures are inevitable. Build in resilience to keep the application stable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a utility function using exponential backoff to handle transient network errors during API requests.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Wrap all API calls in the service layer with a retry decorator using exponential backoff.'

steps.title: Automate Endpoint Testing
steps.description: Eliminate manual testing by generating unit tests for your integration endpoints immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Vitest unit tests for the newly created service layer, mocking the API responses for success and failure cases.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Create unit tests for the service layer and ensure 90% coverage for the API integration logic.'

steps.title: Deploy and Monitor
steps.description: Finalize the integration with automated logging to catch production issues before the client does.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add structured logging to the API service that captures request latency and error codes for observability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Integrate winston logger into the service layer and configure it for production monitoring.'

pro_tips: Always use a dedicated API proxy to hide sensitive keys and prevent CORS issues during development.
Map your API responses to local domain models immediately to prevent logic coupling if the API structure changes.
Use mock servers like MSW during local development to keep building even when the external API is rate-limited.
Implement 'Circuit Breakers' for third-party APIs to prevent your entire app from hanging when a provider goes down.
Treat your API integration code as 'disposable' by keeping it strictly separated from your core business logic.
content.next_steps: Now that your integration is robust, refactor your UI to consume these services. Use the remaining time to implement automated deployment pipelines to ensure your client sees progress every 24 hours.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools to stop stalling and start shipping. Stop letting complex integrations block your growth and start delivering professional-grade software that keeps clients coming back. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
